summary: Parallel review: FAIL (3 critical, 13 warning, 8 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/extension-registry.mjs:9-13` — Singleton ignores `cwd` after first load; future multi-cwd usage (e.g. parallel feature runs) will silently serve stale extensions — key cache on `cwd` or add an assertion comment documenting the single-cwd invariant

🟡 `bin/lib/extension-runner.mjs:38` — `return result` passes through `undefined` when a hook returns nothing; `fireExtension`'s `!== null` filter lets it enter the results array; downstream `r &&` in run.mjs guards it by accident — change filter to `result != null` (loose equality) in `extension-registry.mjs:33` to treat both `null` and `undefined` as "no output"

🟡 `bin/lib/extension-registry.mjs:9` — `cwd` parameter is only honoured on first call; subsequent calls (e.g., second feature in outer-loop mode) silently reuse cached extensions regardless of `cwd` — add a comment documenting the single-cwd invariant, or call `resetRegistry()` at the start of `_runSingleFeature` (analogous to `resetRunUsage()` at `run.mjs:784`)

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1163` — `promptAppend` fires only for the build phase; review (`run.mjs:1231`) and replan (`run.mjs:1419,1469`) call `dispatchToAgent()` without hook invocation — clarify intended scope or extend to all dispatch sites

🟡 `bin/lib/extension-registry.mjs:1` — Extension return contract `{ append: string }` is completely undocumented; extension authors cannot implement the hook without reading source code — add JSDoc or EXTENSIONS.md

🟡 `test/extension-system.test.mjs` — No tests for `extension-loader.mjs`; `safePath` traversal guard and manifest validation are completely untested; add a `describe("loadExtensions")` block covering valid manifest, invalid manifest, traversal attempt, and import error
🟡 `bin/lib/extension-runner.mjs:4` — 5-second timeout is a named design feature but never tested; add a test with a hook that resolves after >5s to confirm it returns `null` and records a failure

🟡 `test/extension-system.test.mjs:198` — "Brief append integration" tests reproduce the append loop inline rather than invoking `run.mjs`; a regression in `run.mjs:1164–1168` (separator, filter condition) won't be caught by these tests

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1231` — `promptAppend` fires only for the build phase; review and replan dispatches at lines 1231, 1419, 1469 skip extensions entirely; this boundary is undocumented and untested — clarify intent with a comment and a test asserting the boundary

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1165` — No size cap on `r.append`; a large string will exceed the OS ARG_MAX limit when passed as a CLI argument to `claude`, or inflate token costs with no bound. Add a per-extension cap (e.g. 10 000 chars) and warn when truncated.

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1163` — Extension-appended content is inserted into a brief dispatched with `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` without sanitization. Although extension code already runs in-process (arbitrary exec by design), a malicious or compromised extension can inject adversarial instructions that the AI agent acts on with elevated implicit authority — distinct from the extension acting directly. Document the trust model or add audit logging for appended content.

🟡 `bin/lib/extension-registry.mjs:22` — `setExtensions()` is exported from the production module. A loaded extension running in the same process can call it to replace the entire registry, bypassing all loader validation. Guard behind `process.env.NODE_ENV === 'test'` or explicitly document that extensions are fully trusted and this is intentional.
🔴 `bin/lib/extension-registry.mjs:9` — `getExtensions` private function used at 1 call site; inline the 3-line lazy-init check directly into `fireExtension`
🔴 `bin/lib/extension-runner.mjs:18` — `recordFailure` private function used at 1 call site; inline `_failures.set(name, (_failures.get(name) ?? 0) + 1)` into the `catch` block
🔴 `bin/lib/extension-loader.mjs:22` — `safePath` private function used at 1 call site; inline the traversal check at line 49 with a `// prevent directory traversal` comment
🟡 `test/extension-system.test.mjs:199` — append loop duplicated inline instead of exercising the real `run.mjs` path; change to filter or separator in `run.mjs` won't be caught
